primary.name: case_study_series_navigation_engagement_rate
primary.definition: unique sessions firing >=1 case_study_series_node_click or _nav_click / unique sessions viewing case-study detail page with series_id

metrics.kill_criteria: Primary kill: if by 30 days post-ship ZERO case_study_series_node_click OR case_study_series_nav_click events fire across ALL 10 series, feature fails its discoverability hypothesis -> rollback to Option 3 (footer related[] only). Performance kill: LCP regression >200ms unfixable in <1d. A11y kill: any new AXE violation unfixable in <1d. Catalog drift kill: >3 catalog inconsistencies within 60 days.

transitions.note: 4 decision points resolved: min cluster size >=3, framework split into hadf + framework-integrity-v7, backfill scope confirmed-needed only (4 MDXs), hybrid naming convention. Final locked series catalog: 10 series, ~66 case studies threaded.

PRD approved as drafted. 12 functional requirements, 4 GA4 events (2 conversion), 4 kill criteria, primary target 5% engagement within 30 days. has_ui=true, requires_analytics=true, analytics_spec_complete=true. Min eval coverage gate auto-passes (no AI-touching code).

Phase 2 approved as drafted. 44 tasks, 5.27 person-days, T24 flagged for isolation. Phase 3 chain: ux research/spec/preflight + design preflight/audit/build. Phase 3 is non-skippable for new UI feature.

Phase 3 complete. ux-research.md + ux-spec.md + ux-preflight-audit + design-preflight + 2 build prompts all written. /ux validate self-check PASS (12/12 checks per spec §12.1). /design audit PASS (compose-primitives build, zero DS evolution). T8 Figma build DEFERRED to Phase 4 close (figma_build_status=deferred_to_prompt). HARD PAUSE before Phase 4 — resume 2026-05-22 post v7.9 promotion decision.

brainstorm.source_record: session transcript: 4 rounds of AskUserQuestion + infra-master-plan overlay

problem.pain: When a project ships across multiple case studies (e.g., UCC: 4 studies spanning v4.3 to v7.8.1, or framework-v7.X: 10 studies from v7.1 to v7.8.6), readers see them as disconnected items in a flat listing. The progression from one part to the next is invisible at the listing level; readers must click into individual studies and manually trace prose mentions to discover the thread.
problem.evidence: fitme-story content-schema.ts:77 has an unused `related: z.array(z.string())` field. Listing page (case-studies/page.tsx) groups only by hardcoded MILESTONES + era buckets + v7 slug-regex categories; no project/feature grouping exists. UCC thread today is invisible: 23a (UCC migration) and 26 (UCC passkey) appear 3 slots apart with no connection cue.
problem.reach: Every reader of the public case-studies listing. ~20-30 case studies belong to multi-part clusters today (4 UCC + 10 framework-v7 + several smaller). Conservative estimate: 50% of public readers care about progression context.
problem.who_complains: External operators / portfolio readers who want to understand how a feature evolved over time.

alternatives_considered.name: Option 1 — Series buckets + breadcrumb (compact)
alternatives_considered.pro: low real estate, scales to many clusters

alternatives_considered.name: Option 2 — Visual timeline thread (prominent) — CHOSEN
alternatives_considered.pro: thread becomes focal UI element on both listing and detail pages, clear chronology + version markers
alternatives_considered.con: higher visual density on listing; ~2-3 days implementation vs ~1 day for Option 1

alternatives_considered.name: Option 3 — Minimal: populated related[] + footer links
alternatives_considered.con: thread only visible AFTER click; defeats discoverability goal

solution.rationale: User explicitly chose visual prominence over compact. They want the thread visible at listing level to signal multi-part progression without requiring a click. Bucket approach was close second but loses visual chronology cue (version markers on horizontal timeline immediately communicates the v4.3 -> v7.8.1 arc for UCC).

assumptions.assumption: A `series_id` frontmatter field is the right schema primitive (vs predecessor/successor pointers, vs a separate catalog file)
assumptions.validation: fitme-story `related[]` field exists as precedent; single ID + chronological sort by `timeline_position.order` is simpler than a graph; combined with a typed catalog file for series-level metadata.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Schema lock-in; harder to express multi-thread membership later (e.g., framework-v7 AND test-discipline). Mitigation: ship single series_id v1, evolve to array if needed.

assumptions.assumption: FT2-only case studies should be backfilled as fitme-story MDX showcases (vs link-out cards)
assumptions.validation: User explicitly chose backfill. Public site becomes canonical thread display.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: 6-10 new MDX authoring effort; risk of stale FT2 .md if MDX summary drifts. Mitigation: MDX links to source FT2 markdown.

assumptions.assumption: Framework v7.X chain is a single 10-part thread (not split by mechanical-enforcement boundary)
assumptions.validation: User explicitly chose single chain. Most truthful to how the work actually unfolded.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: 10-node timeline may be crowded on small viewports. Mitigation: Phase 3 UX spec must handle responsive layout (e.g., collapsed-by-default on mobile, max 5 nodes visible with paging).

assumptions.assumption: Calibration window 2026-05-15 to 2026-05-21 needs protection from this feature's gate fires
assumptions.validation: C1 precedent (cadence-followups.md L133) deferred 9 test fixtures past 2026-05-22 for same reason. Pre-commit gates would fire on case-study MDX edits + state.json phase transitions. Sequencing splits Phases 0-3 (planning, minimal fires) from Phases 4+ (implementation, many fires).
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Either contaminates v7.9 promotion data OR delays feature. Mitigation: hard-pause checkpoint end of Phase 3 — re-evaluate once promotion decision lands 2026-05-21.

strategy.why_now: Public case-study listing matured to 25+ entries; threading would have been premature with <10 studies but now provides real reader value. v7.8.6 shipped 2026-05-15 closed the framework infra plumbing; Phase E window (2026-05-21 to 06-04) is a natural quiet period for product/presentation work.
strategy.why_not_defer: Cluster count grows monotonically; every shipped feature adds zero or one more thread member. Threading retrofit cost grows with cluster size. Better to ship the primitive now so all future case studies benefit automatically.
strategy.why_not_simpler: Minimal option (related[] + footer) was rejected because it doesn't make threads discoverable. Readers would still see a flat listing and learn of threading only post-click. The point of the feature is to make progression visible at the discovery surface.
strategy.kill_criteria_seed: If by 30 days post-ship ZERO external readers click a series-timeline node to navigate between parts (measured via GA4 event), the feature is not solving the discoverability problem. Consider rollback to Option 1 (compact) or Option 3 (footer-only).

paused_reason: v7.9 calibration window protection per cadence-followups.md C1 precedent. Resume Phase 4 (implementation) on 2026-05-22 after v7.9 promotion decision lands 2026-05-21.
